title: Functions, duties and powers of the Election Commission

content.text: The Election Commission shall, subject to this Constitution and the Federal law, conduct, supervise, direct and control the election to the President, Vice-President, members of the Federal Parliament, members of State Assemblies and members of Local level. For these purposes, the Election Commission shall prepare electoral rolls.
The Election Commission shall hold a referendum on a matter of national importance pursuant to this Constitution and the Federal law

If, after nomination of candidacy for the President, Vice- President, member of the Federal Parliament, member of State Assemblies or member of Local level has been filed but before the election results are declared, a question arises about the qualification of a candidate, the Election Commission shall decide that question.

The Election Commission may so delegate any of its functions, duties and powers to the Chief Election Commissioner, Election Commissioner or any government employee as to be exercised and complied with subject to the specified conditions.
Other functions, duties and powers and rules of procedure of the Election Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
